title: mkcert -install gives no hint when nss-tools is missing on dnf-based distros
body: CertutilInstallHelp is only populated when apt, yum, or zypper are detected. On a plain Fedora system without yum (dnf-only), the install fails with a generic certutil-not-found error and no suggested package to install.

title: Firefox profile path not found when USERPROFILE contains non-default drive
body: FirefoxProfiles hardcodes os.Getenv("USERPROFILE") + "\\AppData\\Roaming\\Mozilla\\Firefox\\Profiles", so mkcert -install silently skips the NSS trust store on machines where Firefox stores its profile elsewhere (e.g. a redirected profile folder).

title: Certificate validity period should be configurable
body: makeCert() always sets a 2 year 3 month expiration to stay under Apple's 825-day limit. It would help local dev workflows to allow overriding this via a flag for short-lived certs used in CI.

title: -csr flag interaction with -install is not validated
body: The advanced usage text says -csr conflicts with all other flags except -install and -cert-file, but main.go does not appear to reject -csr combined with -client or -ecdsa; it just silently ignores them.

title: Document java keystore location detection for -install
body: truststore_java.go handles installing the CA into the JVM cacerts store, but there's no mention in the advanced usage text of how mkcert locates JAVA_HOME or which keystores get modified. Would help to add a short note.
